title: The Man Who Saved the World
story: A hero's quick thinking prevented disaster. In 1983, Stanislav Petrov, a Soviet officer, spotted a glitch in the early warning system. It falsely showed incoming missiles! Instead of panicking, Petrov used his judgment and realized it was a mistake. His calm decision likely stopped an accidental nuclear war. Years later, on May 21, 2004, Petrov received the World Citizen Award for his life-saving actions.

title: Amelia Earhart Soars Across the Atlantic
story: Amelia Earhart's plane touched down near Londonderry, Northern Ireland, on May 21, 1932, after an incredible journey. She had just become the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic Ocean! The brave pilot spent 17 hours soaring above the vast, blue waters, starting from Newfoundland. Earhart's daring flight inspired people around the world and showed that women could achieve amazing things in aviation.

title: Atomic Light Guides Ships to Safety
story: A bright idea lit up Chesapeake Bay in 1964. The world's first nuclear-powered lighthouse began its watchful duty on May 21. title: Brain Waves Pioneer Born
story: Zap! Imagine being able to see the electricity in someone's brain. Hans Berger, born in 1873 on May 21, made that possible. This clever German scientist invented a special machine called an electroencephalograph, or EEG for short. It records the tiny electrical signals our brains make. Berger's invention helps doctors understand how our minds work and spot problems. Today, we still use EEGs to peek inside our thinking caps!
activity_question: What did Hans Berger invent?
follow_up_question: Can you guess what EEG stands for?

His brilliant mind and daring spirit helped launch the US aviation industry, earning him the nickname Father of Naval Aviation.

title: Steam Power Reaches Paradise
story: Puffs of smoke appeared on the horizon as a marvel of technology approached Hawaii's shores. In 1846, the first steamship ever to reach the islands chugged into view on May 21. This historic arrival marked a new era of faster travel and trade for the tropical paradise, connecting it more closely with the rest of the world.
activity_question: What type of ship first arrived in Hawaii on May 21, 1846?
bonus_fact: Before steamships, sailing vessels took much longer to reach Hawaii, often weeks or months.

title: A Lonely Island Found
story: Sailing across the vast South Atlantic Ocean, Portuguese explorer João Da Nova made an exciting discovery. On May 21, 1502, his ship stumbled upon a remote, uninhabited island. This lush green speck in the endless blue sea was Saint Helena. No people lived there, just plants and animals. It became an important stop for ships on long voyages.
activity_question: Who discovered Saint Helena island?
follow_up_question: Why do you think finding uninhabited islands was important for early explorers?

title: A Girl's Daring Journey
story: Mary Campbell's life changed forever in 1758. Lenape warriors swept into her Pennsylvania home, taking the young girl captive during the French and Indian War. On May 21, Mary began an unexpected adventure that would shape her future. She lived with the Lenape for years, learning their ways and language.
activity_question: Who took Mary Campbell captive?
follow_up_question: What war was happening when Mary was taken?
